System instructions:

You are summarizing a verified Lean theorem as a concrete supporting informal sublemma. Return only JSON matching the schema.

User task:

Theorem title: Weak formulation of the Poisson problem via Lax–Milgram

Parent informal node:

{
  "id": "n1",
  "title": "Boundedness of the Dirichlet bilinear form",
  "informal_statement": "For all \\(u,v\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\),\n\\[\n|a(u,v)|=\\left|\\int_\\Omega \\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla v\\,dx\\right|\n\\le \\|u\\|_{H_0^1(\\Omega)}\\,\\|v\\|_{H_0^1(\\Omega)}.\n\\]\nHence \\(a\\) is bounded on \\(H_0^1(\\Omega)\\times H_0^1(\\Omega)\\).",
  "informal_proof_text": "Apply Cauchy\u2013Schwarz to the integral of \\(\\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla v\\):\n\\[\n\\left|\\int_\\Omega \\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla v\\,dx\\right|\n\\le \\left(\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx\\right)^{1/2}\n\\left(\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla v|^2\\,dx\\right)^{1/2}.\n\\]\nOn \\(H_0^1(\\Omega)\\), this is exactly the \\(H_0^1\\)-norm bound used here."
}

Verified Lean sublemma:

{
  "lean_theorem_name": "dirichlet_form_bounded",
  "lean_statement": "theorem dirichlet_form_bounded\n    {d : \u2115}\n    (\u03a9 : Set (EuclideanSpace \u211d (Fin d)))\n    (grad_u grad_v :\n      MeasureTheory.Lp (EuclideanSpace \u211d (Fin d)) 2 (volume.restrict \u03a9)) :\n    |\u222b x, \u27eagrad_u x, grad_v x\u27eb \u2202(volume.restrict \u03a9)| \u2264 \u2016grad_u\u2016 * \u2016grad_v\u2016"
}

Write a short informal statement and a short informal proof text for a supporting local sublemma that matches the verified Lean theorem. Keep it concrete, close to the parent node's mathematical setting, and honest about being narrower than the parent node if it is narrower.

Do not introduce arbitrary ambient abstraction not already present in the Lean theorem. Do not claim this sublemma proves the whole parent node.

Formatting guidance: use LaTeX math delimiters like \(...\) or \[...\] for mathematical expressions. Use backticks only for literal Lean identifiers, theorem names, or simple variable names such as `horth` or `grad_u`. Do not put LaTeX commands like \int, \lVert, \Omega, \langle, or \cdot inside backticks.

Return a JSON object with keys informal_statement and informal_proof_text.

raw_stdout:
{"informal_statement":"For measurable vector fields `grad_u` and `grad_v` on \(\Omega\) that belong to \(L^2(\Omega)\),
\[
\left|\int_\Omega \langle \mathrm{grad\_u}(x), \mathrm{grad\_v}(x)\rangle \, dx\right|
\le \|\mathrm{grad\_u}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\,\|\mathrm{grad\_v}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}.
\]
This is the boundedness estimate for the Dirichlet integrand at the level of the two gradient fields themselves.","informal_proof_text":"Apply the Cauchy--Schwarz inequality in \(L^2(\Omega)\) to the inner product integrand \(\langle \mathrm{grad\_u}(x), \mathrm{grad\_v}(x)\rangle\). This gives
\[
\left|\int_\Omega \langle \mathrm{grad\_u}(x), \mathrm{grad\_v}(x)\rangle \, dx\right|
\le \left(\int_\Omega |\mathrm{grad\_u}(x)|^2\,dx\right)^{1/2}
\left(\int_\Omega |\mathrm{grad\_v}(x)|^2\,dx\right)^{1/2}.
\]
By definition, these square roots are exactly \(\|\mathrm{grad\_u}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\) and \(\|\mathrm{grad\_v}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\)."}

payload.informal_statement:
For measurable vector fields `grad_u` and `grad_v` on \(\Omega\) that belong to \(L^2(\Omega)\),
\[
\left|\int_\Omega \langle \mathrm{grad\_u}(x), \mathrm{grad\_v}(x)\rangle \, dx\right|
\le \|\mathrm{grad\_u}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\,\|\mathrm{grad\_v}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}.
\]
This is the boundedness estimate for the Dirichlet integrand at the level of the two gradient fields themselves.
payload.informal_proof_text:
Apply the Cauchy--Schwarz inequality in \(L^2(\Omega)\) to the inner product integrand \(\langle \mathrm{grad\_u}(x), \mathrm{grad\_v}(x)\rangle\). This gives
\[
\left|\int_\Omega \langle \mathrm{grad\_u}(x), \mathrm{grad\_v}(x)\rangle \, dx\right|
\le \left(\int_\Omega |\mathrm{grad\_u}(x)|^2\,dx\right)^{1/2}
\left(\int_\Omega |\mathrm{grad\_v}(x)|^2\,dx\right)^{1/2}.
\]
By definition, these square roots are exactly \(\|\mathrm{grad\_u}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\) and \(\|\mathrm{grad\_v}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\).
